title: Phelipanche olbiensis
taxonomy: Phelipanche olbiensis is a species of holoparasitic plant. It belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. Phelipanche olbiensis is a member of the genus Phelipanche, which comprises a group of root-parasitic plants. These plants obtain their nutrients by connecting to the roots of their host plants, typically members of the carrot family. The classification of Phelipanche olbiensis reflects its unique and specialized parasitic lifestyle.
uses: Phelipanche olbiensis has been found to have potential medicinal uses, including its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory effects, which may be useful in treating various health conditions. Research on Phelipanche olbiensis is ongoing, and its full range of medicinal applications is not yet fully understood. Further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and potential uses in traditional medicine.

parts: Phelipanche olbiensis is a parasitic plant, and the parts used in traditional medicine are the roots and aerial parts. side_effects: Phelipanche olbiensis, a plant species known to be highly toxic and invasive, can cause severe side effects in humans and animals.
